rules.title: Name the visual direction
rules.instruction: Before implementation, state the visual direction in one concrete sentence. It must describe composition, typography, color behavior, and emotional register. Avoid mood-word soup.
rules.rationale: A named direction prevents the model from blending unrelated trends into a generic interface.
verify: The direction could guide a second designer without screenshots.
Every major visual choice supports the same direction.

rules.title: Establish a small token system
rules.instruction: Define reusable tokens for type scale, spacing, color roles, radius, and depth before multiplying components. Prefer a deliberate small system over scattered one-off values.
verify: Repeated values are represented by tokens.
Token names describe roles rather than raw appearance.

rules.title: Do not default to the model's favorite font
rules.instruction: Do not choose Inter, Roboto, Arial, or system-ui as the primary display face without an explicit reason. Select typography that supports the content and brand, then pair it with a readable text face.
rules.rationale: The problem is unexamined defaulting, not the fonts themselves.
verify: Display typography has a documented role.
Body text remains readable across target viewports.

detect.message: Generic primary font detected. Keep it only when the design rationale explicitly calls for it.

rules.title: Reject decorative purple-gradient defaulting
rules.instruction: Do not use a purple or indigo gradient as generic technological decoration. A gradient is acceptable only when it carries brand meaning, hierarchy, state, or spatial function.
verify: The gradient has a stated job beyond making the interface look 'AI'.

detect.message: Purple or indigo gradient heuristic matched. Confirm that it serves a real design function.

rules.title: Do not center every decision
rules.instruction: Avoid stacking a centered headline, centered paragraph, centered buttons, and centered decorative art by reflex. Choose alignment and asymmetry based on reading flow and focal hierarchy.
verify: The composition has an intentional reading path.
Alignment changes are structural, not random.

detect.message: Repeated centered-layout utilities detected. Review whether the whole composition has collapsed into a template.

rules.title: Use one depth language
rules.instruction: Do not combine large soft shadows, glows, glass blur, borders, and floating cards on every surface. Choose one dominant depth language and reserve stronger elevation for meaningful hierarchy.
verify: Elevation levels map to semantic importance.
Most surfaces remain visually quiet.

detect.message: Large shadow or glow heuristic matched. Verify that depth is scarce and hierarchical.

rules.title: Do not turn every surface into a pill
rules.instruction: Use radius as part of the product's shape language. Avoid large rounded corners on every card, control, image, panel, and badge unless the direction explicitly demands it.
verify: Radius values come from tokens.
Different component roles do not all collapse into the same blob shape.

detect.message: Large-radius utility detected. Review aggregate usage across the page.

rules.title: Animate named properties
rules.instruction: Do not animate every property. Name the properties that communicate state, preserve reduced-motion behavior, and keep motion subordinate to interaction.
verify: Animations have a user-facing purpose.
prefers-reduced-motion is respected where motion is nonessential.

detect.message: transition-all or transition: all detected. Animate only the properties that should move.

rules.title: Preserve visible keyboard focus
rules.instruction: Never remove focus indication without replacing it with an equally visible focus-visible treatment that meets contrast requirements.
verify: Every interactive element can be located with keyboard focus.
Focus treatment is visible in light and dark contexts.

detect.message: Focus outline is disabled. Add an explicit, visible :focus-visible replacement.

rules.title: Ban placeholder hero language
rules.instruction: Replace abstract claims with a concrete promise, mechanism, or proof. Avoid interchangeable hero phrases that could headline any AI startup.
verify: The headline identifies a specific outcome or tension.
The supporting copy explains how the product earns the claim.

detect.message: Generic hero phrase detected. Replace it with product-specific language and proof.

patterns.source: (revolutionize your|unlock the power of|elevate your|supercharge your|future of innovation|next generation of)

rules.title: Protect reading measure
rules.instruction: Keep long-form body text within a deliberate reading measure, generally near 55 to 75 characters per line, and provide enough line-height for sustained reading.
verify: Article paragraphs do not span the full viewport.
Body size and leading remain comfortable on mobile and desktop.

rules.title: Set density from task frequency
rules.instruction: Choose spacing and control density from task frequency and information load. Do not apply spacious marketing-page rhythm to operational interfaces or cram occasional workflows into permanent dashboard density.
verify: Primary repeated tasks are fast to scan and operate.
Low-frequency actions do not dominate permanent space.

rules.title: Protect one primary action
rules.instruction: Each major section should have one obvious primary action. Secondary links may exist, but competing button styles must not flatten the hierarchy.
verify: A first-time visitor can identify the intended next step within seconds.
Primary action styling remains consistent across the page.

rules.title: Form controls must have an accessible name
rules.instruction: Every input, textarea, and select element must have an accessible name. Provide it via a <label> element, aria-label, or aria-labelledby. Do not rely on placeholder text as the sole identifier.
rules.rationale: Placeholder text disappears on input, leaving users without a referent. WCAG requires every interactive control to have an accessible name. A label element is preferred because it provides both a visible label and an accessible name.
verify: Every form control has a <label for=""> association, wrapping label, aria-label, or aria-labelledby.
Controls labeled by placeholder text alone are not compliant.

detect.message: No supported static accessible-name source was found for this form control.

rules.title: Provide prefers-reduced-motion overrides for CSS motion
rules.instruction: When using CSS animation or transition properties, provide a corresponding override inside @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) that disables or neutralizes the motion for the same selector.
rules.rationale: Users with vestibular disorders can experience discomfort from motion. WCAG 2.3.3 (Animation from Interactions, Level AAA) requires a mechanism to pause or hide non-essential motion. A static prefers-reduced-motion override is the standard CSS technique.
verify: Every selector with animation or transition properties has a matching selector inside @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) with a disabling override.
Disabling overrides include: animation: none, transition: none, animation-duration: 0s, transition-duration: 0s.

Note: Declarations are analyzed per block. Longhands split across duplicate selector blocks (e.g. .x { animation-name: spin; } and .x { animation-duration: 1s; }) are not aggregated. Combine related motion declarations into a single selector block.

detect.message: No supported static reduced-motion override was found for this selector.

rules.title: Provide a static skip link to main content
rules.instruction: Include a same-document anchor as one of the first focusable elements that targets the main-content region. The link must have a supported accessible name (visible text, aria-label, or aria-labelledby) and resolve to a <main> element or an element with role="main" in the same file. Static analysis cannot verify runtime focus behavior or CSS visibility.
rules.rationale: Keyboard users need a mechanism to bypass repeated navigation blocks. A skip link targeting the main-content region is the most widely supported technique. Static analysis can confirm the presence of a supported skip-link structure but cannot prove runtime keyboard behavior or complete WCAG 2.4.1 (Bypass Blocks) conformance.
verify: The page contains an <a href="#..."> element before the first <nav> element, with visible link text or aria-label.
The fragment target exists in the same file and is a <main> element or an element with role="main".
Note: Static analysis does not verify CSS visibility on focus, client-side routing, JavaScript focus management, or alternative bypass mechanisms such as ARIA landmarks.

detect.message: No supported static skip link targeting a main-content region was found.

rules.title: Require minimum rendered text contrast
rules.instruction: Ensure rendered text reaches the applicable minimum contrast ratio in the intended viewport and color scheme.
rules.rationale: Low-contrast text can be difficult to read for people with low vision, reduced contrast sensitivity, or color-vision differences.
verify: Normal text measures at least 4.5:1 against its rendered background.
Large-scale text measures at least 3:1 against its rendered background.
Unsupported visual composition is reviewed manually rather than treated as a pass.

detect.message: Rendered text contrast measured below the configured minimum in the analyzed Chromium state.

rules.title: Require minimum rendered touch-target size
rules.instruction: Ensure rendered pointer targets meet the minimum size or a confirmed spacing exception in the intended viewport.
rules.rationale: Small or densely packed targets can be difficult to activate accurately for users with limited dexterity or imprecise pointer input.
verify: Supported targets contain an axis-aligned 24 by 24 CSS-pixel area.
Undersized targets satisfy the official spacing-circle exception.
Unsupported geometry remains indeterminate rather than being treated as a pass.

detect.message: Rendered interactive target measured below the configured minimum in the analyzed Chromium state.
